endpoints.name: Send data to a feed via webhook URL.
endpoints.description: Send data to a feed via webhook URL.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY.

endpoints.name: Send arbitrary data to a feed via webhook URL.
endpoints.description: The raw data webhook receiver accepts POST requests and stores the raw request body on your feed. This is useful when you don't have control of the webhook sender. If feed history is turned on, payloads will be truncated at 1024 bytes. If feed history is turned off, payloads will be truncated at 100KB.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY.

endpoints.name: Create new Data
endpoints.description:
Create new data records on the given feed.

**NOTE:** when feed history is on, data `value` size is limited to 1KB, when feed history is turned off data value size is limited to 100KB.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Create multiple new Data records
endpoints.description: Create multiple new Data records
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Chart data for current feed
endpoints.description:
The Chart API is what we use on io.adafruit.com to populate charts over varying timespans with a consistent number of data points. The maximum number of points returned is 480. This API works by aggregating slices of time into a single value by averaging.

All time-based parameters are optional, if none are given it will default to 1 hour at the finest-grained resolution possible.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: First Data in Queue
endpoints.description: Get the oldest data point in the feed. This request sets the queue pointer to the beginning of the feed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Last Data in Queue
endpoints.description: Get the most recent data point in the feed. This request sets the queue pointer to the end of the feed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Next Data in Queue
endpoints.description: Get the next newest data point in the feed. If queue processing hasn't been started, the first data point in the feed will be returned.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Previous Data in Queue
endpoints.description: Get the previously processed data point in the feed. NOTE: this method doesn't move the processing queue pointer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.

endpoints.name: Last Data in MQTT CSV format
endpoints.description: Get the most recent data point in the feed in an MQTT compatible CSV format: `value,lat,lon,ele`
endpoints.notes: Replace {{ADAFRUIT_API_KEY}} with your ADAFRUIT API KEY. Replace {{FEED_KEY}} with a real feed_key. Replace {{USERNAME}} with a real username.
